title: NINTH GRADE COUNTS - 211info
text: LATINO NETWORK NINTH GRADE COUNTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Summer enrichment program for incoming freshmen transitioning into high school. Students learn about time management, internships, writing scholarship essays, how to succeed in high school, career exploration, healthy sexuality, and Latin American culture. 410 NE 18th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Incoming Latino freshmen transitioning into 9th grade Hours: Daily for 4 weeks during summer Email (503) 283-6881 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 283-6881 [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Youth Enrichment Programs * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - ADVOCACY EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - JUNTOS AL KINDER EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS/SCHOOLS - JUNTOS APRENDEMOS EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - COLEGIO DE PADRES/PARENTS COLLEGE EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY ESCALERA EDUCATIONAL SUPPORT SERVICES - ESCALERA INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CRECIENDO JUNTOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - MILPA GUN VIOLENCE PREVENTION PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PADRINOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SOÑANDO JUNTOS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - UNIDOS FOR OREGON LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - COMMUNITY HEALING INITIATIVE LEGAL SERVICES - PRESCHOOL FOR ALL NAVIGATOR SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CONEXIONES SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - P3 COORDINATORS UTILITIES - CITY OF PORTLAND WATER DISCOUNT PROGRAM UTILITIES - ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge.
